title: Meathead: The Science of Great Barbecue and Grilling
author: Meathead Goldwyn (with Prof. Greg Blonder)
note: Catalog built from the table of contents (titles + page numbers only). To add any of these as a real cookwhat recipe, scan the full recipe page(s) so the actual ingredient list + method can be captured. Now covers Chicken & Turkey (ch.10), Seafood (ch.11), and Sides (ch.12). COMPLETE: full TOC captured through the end of the Sides chapter (book ends with Index, p.367).
source_format: Source for any meal cataloged from this book: "Meathead (Goldwyn) — p.NNN" (no URL).

recipes.title: Big Bad Beef Rub
recipes.fitsMenu: saved for a future brisket/ribs cook (pulled off the ribeye - too bold for a quick steak)

ingredients: 2 tsp chili powder or ground ancho
captured.method: Mix together in a bowl. No salt in the rub - salt the meat separately.

ai.cliffNotes: A bold spice rub that layers heat, smoke, and umami without salt, which is added to the meat separately to prevent drawing out moisture during cooking.
keyTips: Salt the meat separately from the rub to avoid extracting surface moisture that inhibits browning
Coarsely grind the black pepper yourself rather than using pre-ground for more vibrant pepper flavor and texture
Mix the dry spices thoroughly to ensure even distribution, especially the potent chipotle and garlic powder which can clump

recipes.title: Simon & Garfunkel Rub
recipes.fitsMenu: herb rub for poultry; used by Grilled Asparagus & Cornell Chicken

captured.method: Pulse everything in a blender or food processor a few seconds until you have a coarse powder. All-purpose rub for pork, turkey, chicken, veggies, eggs, grilled potatoes, asparagus.

ai.cliffNotes: Combine dried herbs with sugar and pepper, then pulse briefly to create a coarse, aromatic powder that clings to proteins and vegetables while maintaining textural interest.
keyTips: Pulse only a few seconds to maintain coarse texture — over-processing creates fine powder that doesn't adhere as well and tastes dusty
Crush dried herbs by hand before blending to break down larger pieces and ensure more even distribution in the final rub
Apply rub generously to slightly damp meat or vegetables so it adheres better during cooking rather than falling off
Store in an airtight container away from heat and light to preserve the volatile oils in the herbs that provide flavor

recipes.title: Hawaiian Huli-Huli Teriyaki Sauce and Marinade
Roasted Red Pepper and Garlic Coulis

ingredients: 2 tsp finely chopped fresh oregano (or 1 tsp dried)
captured.method: Combine in a lidded jar and shake thoroughly; shake again just before using. Also good with fresh parsley, basil, or fennel.

ai.cliffNotes: Emulsify olive oil and lemon juice through vigorous shaking to create a temporary suspension that coats seafood evenly.
keyTips: Use high-quality extra-virgin olive oil—its flavor is essential since there are so few ingredients
Shake the jar vigorously and again just before serving, as the emulsion will separate and you need fresh coating action
Fresh lemon juice matters; bottled lacks the brightness needed to balance the oil
Apply immediately after shaking while the emulsion is still suspended, before it breaks

recipes.title: Pork Tenderloin with Cowboy Java Rub
Big, Thick Steakhouse Steaks
recipes.fitsMenu: the technique for the July 4 ribeye

recipes.title: The Afterburner Method for Skinny Steaks
Santa Maria Tri-Tip (Poor Man's Prime Rib)

captured.method: Soften the butter, mash in the herbs with a fork, roll in plastic wrap like a Tootsie Roll (~1 inch thick), twist the ends, and chill overnight. Keeps in fridge or freezer. Endlessly variable - add chipotle, mushrooms, port, miso, etc.

ai.cliffNotes: Soften butter, fold in herbs, and roll tightly in plastic wrap before chilling to create a compound butter that melts over hot food.
keyTips: Soften butter to room temperature so herbs incorporate evenly without breaking down
Roll tightly in plastic wrap and twist ends firmly to create a uniform cylinder that slices cleanly
Chill overnight to let flavors meld and butter firm up enough to hold its shape when sliced
Slice into rounds and place on hot meat or vegetables immediately after cooking so the butter melts into sauce

recipes.title: Simon & Garfunkel Chicken
recipes.fitsMenu: alt for the Fri grilled chicken

captured.method: Whisk the egg, oil, vinegar, salt, S&G rub, and pepper into a marinade; marinate the chicken quarters 3-24 hours. Grill indirect (~325F), flipping, until breasts hit 160F and thighs 165F.

ai.cliffNotes: An emulsified vinegar-oil marinade with egg creates a basting liquid that keeps chicken moist during indirect grilling while building flavor.
keyTips: Marinate 3-24 hours for the acidity to penetrate and tenderize, but don't exceed 24 hours or the texture becomes mushy
Use indirect heat at 325°F to avoid burning the oil-based marinade while allowing even cooking
Monitor internal temps carefully (160°F breast, 165°F thigh) since the marinade and flipping can mask doneness visually
Reserve some marinade before adding raw chicken to use as a finishing baste in the final minutes of grilling

recipes.title: Sweet Georgia Brown Smoked Yard Bird
Big Bob Gibson's Chicken in 'Bama White Sauce
Grilled Duck Breasts in Cherry-Port Sauce
Chef Bonner's Fish Fillets with Brioni Broth

ingredients: 1 lb skinless mild white fish (snapper, mahi-mahi, grouper, or tilapia)
1 tbsp mayonnaise (plus more to coat the fish)
2 1/2 tsp finely chopped chipotle in adobo
pico de gallo or red salsa
captured.method: Season fish with salt, ancho, and pepper. Mix sour cream + mayo + chipotle; chill. Coat fish in a thin layer of mayo and grill on a metal topper over two-zone heat until just cooked. Warm tortillas; toss avocado with lime. Assemble: fish, chipotle crema, cabbage, avocado, pico; serve with lime wedges.

ai.cliffNotes: Mild white fish is seasoned simply with chile and pepper, grilled quickly with a mayo barrier to prevent sticking, then finished with a chipotle-spiked crema that carries the heat and richness.
keyTips: Use a metal grill topper and coat fish lightly with mayo before grilling—this prevents sticking and helps the fish stay moist
Don't overcook the fish; it's done the moment it flakes easily—overcooking dries out mild white fish instantly
Toss avocado with lime juice just before assembly to prevent browning and add brightness to cut through the richness of the crema
Mix the chipotle crema ahead and chill it so flavors meld and it's ready to serve, speeding up final assembly

recipes.title: Grilled Oysters with White Wine Sauce
Championship Bacon-Wrapped Stuffed Shrimp
recipes.flag: cream cheese (dairy); it's an appetizer, not a dinner main

ingredients: 20 shell-on large (16/20 count) shrimp, preferably wild Gulf
Thai sweet chili sauce (e.g. Maggi Taste of Asia Mild Sweet Chili)
finely chopped chives or flat-leaf parsley (optional garnish)
captured.method: Peel/devein shrimp leaving tails on; brine (p.343). Halve and seed jalapenos; stuff each shrimp with cream cheese and a jalapeno strip, wrap in bacon, secure with a toothpick. Grill two-zone (~325F indirect) until the bacon is done; brush with sweet chili sauce and garnish.

ai.cliffNotes: Brining the shrimp before stuffing ensures they stay tender and juicy while the bacon renders and crisps over indirect heat.
keyTips: Brine the peeled shrimp first to season throughout and prevent them from drying out during grilling
Use the two-zone setup so bacon renders fully without overcooking the delicate shrimp meat
Secure each bundle tightly with a toothpick so the bacon stays wrapped and doesn't unroll during cooking
Don't skip leaving the tails on—they provide a handle and help shrimp cook evenly by keeping the shape intact

recipes.title: Grilled Asparagus
recipes.fitsMenu: the green side for the July 4 ribeye

ingredients: 1 tbsp balsamic condimento or balsamic reduction
captured.method: Snap off the woody bottoms (1-3 in). Coat spears with oil, sprinkle with the rub and salt, shave the cheese into wide ribbons. Grill two-zone ~325F on the indirect side; finish with the Parm ribbons and a balsamic drizzle.

ai.cliffNotes: Two-zone grilling with indirect heat allows asparagus to cook through without charring, while the finishing touch of Parmigiano ribbons and balsamic adds richness and acidity.
keyTips: Snap woody bottoms before cooking—they won't soften and waste grill space
Use two-zone setup with indirect side as primary cooking zone to avoid burning the delicate spears
Add cheese and balsamic after grilling when spears are hot so Parm softens slightly and flavors meld
Shave cheese into wide ribbons rather than grating for better texture and visual appeal
